description: A dismissible inline status banner with an X close button. Compose `Alert` + status icon + an `<AlertTitle>` / `<AlertDescription>` + a ghost `Button` for the close action.

meta.docs:
# InlineNotification

A dismissible inline status banner with an X close button. Compose `Alert` + status icon + an `<AlertTitle>` / `<AlertDescription>` + a ghost `Button` for the close action.

## When to use

- **Dismissible error** ("Could not save — try again") — `<Alert variant="destructive">` + `<XCircle />` + your own dismiss button.
- **Dismissible info** (new version available, system notice the user can close) — default `<Alert>` + `<Info />`.
- **Dismissible success** — `<Alert>` with green Tailwind classes + `<CheckCircle2 />`. No built-in success variant.
- **Dismissible warning** — `<Alert>` with amber classes + `<AlertTriangle />`. No built-in warning variant.
- **Auto-dismiss after a delay** — wrap state with `useEffect` + `setTimeout` (Carbon's `timeout` is not built in).
- **Non-dismissible status** — render `<Alert>` without the close button (no separate prop needed).

## When not to use

- **Permanent contextual notice with no dismiss button** — use the `Callout` migration pattern (same `Alert` primitive, no close button).
- **Status with a primary CTA inside** — use the `ActionableNotification` migration pattern (`Alert` + `Button` + custom dismiss).
- **Transient toast feedback** — use `Sonner`.
- **Blocking confirmation** — use `AlertDialog`.

## Composition

- Three Carbon notifications (`Callout`, `InlineNotification`, `ActionableNotification`) all map to **the same shadcn primitive — `Alert`**. The differences are pure composition: dismiss button presence, CTA presence, status colour mapping.
- Open/closed state is yours: track with `useState`, return `null` (or animate) when closed.
- Status icon and colour for non-`destructive` kinds are not built in. Apply Tailwind classes ad-hoc, or extend `alertVariants` with `success`/`warning`/`info` entries if reused across the app.
- For multiple call-sites, extract a tiny app-local `<DismissibleAlert kind title subtitle />` helper that wraps the `useState` + close-button pattern.
meta.migrationGuide: # InlineNotification — Carbon → shadcn migration guide ## Where it sits among the notification family Carbon ships three closely-related notification components: - **`Callout`** — permanent, non-dismissible, no close button (covered separately). - **`InlineNotification`** — dismissible by default, has an X close button. **This guide.** - **`ActionableNotification`** — like `InlineNotification` but with a CTA button (covered separately). shadcn ships **one** primitive — `Alert` — and you compose dismissibility, status icons, and the CTA button yourself. The canonical Alert lives at `alert/alert.shadcn.tsx`; this wrapper re-exports it. ## Structural differences Carbon's `InlineNotification` is **monolithic**: pass `kind`, `title`, `subtitle`, `hideCloseButton`, `onCloseButtonClick`, and the component renders the icon + status colour + close button + state. shadcn's `Alert` is **decomposed**: assemble the icon, title, description, and (optionally) close button yourself. Track open/closed state in your component. | Concept | Carbon | shadcn | |---|---|---| | Wrapper | `` | `…` | | Icon | implicit (chosen from `kind`) | render manually as a child | | Title | `title` prop | `` | | Subtitle / body | `subtitle` prop | `` | | Close button | built-in (X icon, top-right) | render `` inside the Alert. Return `null` when `!open`. 5. For `hideCloseButton`: just don't render the close button — no prop needed. 6. For `lowContrast`: add `className="border-transparent bg-muted"` to mute the visual weight. 7. For `timeout`: add `useEffect(() => { const t = setTimeout(() => setOpen(false), timeout); return () => clearTimeout(t); }, [timeout])`. 8. If multiple callsites: extract a tiny app-local `` helper that takes `kind`/`title`/`subtitle` and handles open state internally.
